problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.prompt:
Given a string s containing only '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', return true if the input string is valid.

A string is valid if: open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets, and open brackets are closed in the correct order.

problems.title: Minimum Stack Design
problems.prompt:
Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.

Implement MinStack with:
- push(val)
- pop()
- top()
- getMin()

All operations must be O(1).
constraints: Methods are called on non-empty stacks

samples.input: push -2, push 0, push -3, getMin, pop, top, getMin

problems.title: Daily Temperatures
problems.prompt: Given an array of integers temperatures where temperatures[i] is the daily temperature on day i, return an array answer such that answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait after day i to get a warmer temperature. If there is no future day for which this is possible, keep answer[i] = 0.

problems.title: Next Greater Element
problems.prompt: Given a circular integer array nums (the next element of nums[nums.length - 1] is nums[0]), return the next greater number for every element. The next greater number of a number x is the first greater number to its traversing-order next in the array; wrap around if needed. If it doesn't exist, return -1 for that number.

problems.title: Decode String
problems.prompt: Given an encoded string, return its decoded string. The encoding rule is: k[encoded_string], where the encoded_string inside the square brackets is being repeated exactly k times. k is guaranteed to be a positive integer. You may assume the input is always valid with no extra whitespace.
constraints: s consists of lowercase letters, digits, and square brackets
s is guaranteed to be a valid encoding

problems.title: Asteroid Collision
problems.prompt:
We are given an array asteroids of integers representing asteroids in a row. For each asteroid, the absolute value represents its size, and the sign represents its direction (positive = right, negative = left). All asteroids move at the same speed.

Find out the state of the asteroids after all collisions. If two asteroids meet, the smaller one explodes; if equal, both explode. Two asteroids moving in the same direction never meet.

problems.title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
problems.prompt:
Given an array of integers heights representing the histogram's bar height where the width of each bar is 1, return the area of the largest rectangle in the histogram.

Constraints are modest (n <= 10^4) so a monotonic-stack O(n) solution is expected.

problems.title: Basic Calculator II
problems.prompt: Given a string expression representing an expression of integers and operators +, -, *, / (integer division toward zero), return the result. The expression contains only non-negative integers, +, -, *, /, and spaces. Assume the expression is always valid and there are no parentheses.
constraints: s consists of integers and operators + - * / and spaces
All intermediate results fit in a 32-bit integer
